commands.repair_reason: No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed: the patch is already focused and has no actionable findings, while the protected maintainer label leaves merge handling to maintainers.; Cleared: No dependency, workflow, credential, permission, or external-code execution changes were introduced; the patch only preserves existing untrusted inbound context in the model prompt path. commands.review_summary:
The branch preserves current inbound reply context in runtime-only embedded prompts, treats reply-target context as current-turn input for bare mention auto replies, adds regression tests, and adds a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main drops params.currentInboundContext on runtime-only turns, and the linked report gives bare Telegram mention reply steps that enter that empty-transcript path. I did not execute the live repro locally because this read-only review cannot run artifact-generating tests or services.
commands.review_followup: No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed: the patch is already focused and has no actionable findings, while the protected maintainer label leaves merge handling to maintainers.

target.title: fix(agents): preserve bare Telegram reply context
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #83767.

This preserves reply-target/current inbound context for bare Telegram mention replies that enter the runtime-only prompt path. The embedded runner now keeps `currentInboundContext` attached to runtime-only model prompts, and the auto-reply path treats reply-target context as real current-turn input without changing explicit `/new` or `/reset` behavior.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.media-only.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts` (`3` files, `151` tests passed)
- `AUTOREVIEW_AUTO_TESTS=0 .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` (clean: no accepted/actionable findings)

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Telegram bare mention replies to an earlier bot message keep the replied-to message body in the model-visible prompt.

Real environment tested: AWS Crabbox provider `aws`, lease `cbx_dea94cd93cc6`, run `run_8e23f39d9aa6`, live Telegram group with the OpenClaw Telegram bot and a real human reply.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: started the patched OpenClaw gateway on AWS Crabbox with a mock OpenAI `/v1/responses` server, seeded Telegram message `247` containing `ISSUE_83767_CLEAN_FIX_PROOF_1779160268966`, then replied directly to that Telegram message.

Evidence after fix: the captured model request to `/v1/responses` contained `ISSUE_83767_CLEAN_FIX_PROOF_1779160268966`, `Conversation context`, `[reply target]`, `Current message:`, `[Replying to: "ISSUE_83767_CLEAN_FIX_PROOF_1779160268966"]`, and the bare mention.

Observed result after fix: the live proof script classified the request as fixed and exited `0` with `promptIncludesReplyTargetText`, `promptIncludesTelegramCurrentMessage`, `promptIncludesBareMention`, `promptIncludesReplyingTo`, and `promptIncludesConversationReplyTarget` all true.

What was not tested: I did not run a full repository test suite; validation was scoped to the affected auto-reply and embedded runner surfaces plus the live Telegram repro path.
target.author: joshavant
